Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of The Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: MOTION OF DEFENDANTS MICHAEL BEAUDRY AND JACQUELINE HARTWELL TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Michael Beaudry and Jacqueline Hartwell's motion to seal portions of motion for summary judgment/adjudication papers is denied. Defendants' only support for this motion is that certain documents have been designated confidential and conclusory statements in an attorney declaration. A sealing order must be supported by a "specific showing" of "facts" as to all the factors in CRC 2.550(d). (McNair v. NCAA (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 25, 35). Parties' agreement to designate documents as confidential, much less one side's unilateral decision to do so, is not a sufficient basis for a sealing order. (CRC 2.551(a)). Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SAMUEL RAY, Esq. of Colla & Ray LLP (tel: 415-579-1414) for Plaintiff.
Atty. ANDREW MACKAY, Esq. of Donahue Fitzgerald LLP (510-451-3300) for Defendants Michael Beudry and Jacqueline Hartwell.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEAUDRY, JACQUELINE HARTWELL's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court rules as follows on defendants' (Beaudry and Hartwell) demurrer to the first amended complaint:
Per CCP 452, plaintiff's alter ego allegations are sufficient.
The court rejects defendant's misjoinder argument and overrules the demurrers to the conversion and breach of contract causes of action.
The demurrer to the breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.
The demurrer to the "Unjust Enrichment/Restitution" cause of action is overruled.
The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.
The demurrer to the unfair business practice claim is overruled.
(See word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL BEAUDRY, JACQUELINE HARTWELL DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

The demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires a pre-demurrer meet-and-confer each time a pleading is amended. (Id. at (a).) Counsel may not agree otherwise; enforcement of CCP 430.41 is for the court. The new response date to the amended complaint is February 6, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
